query: Turkey EPIAS TEIAS electricity generation May 2026 monthly data wind solar coal
answer:
I now have a comprehensive dataset. Let me compile the final research report.

---

# Turkey EPIAS/TEIAS Electricity Generation — May 2026 Monthly Data: Wind, Solar, Coal

## Key Finding: May 2026 Data Not Yet Published

**May 2026 monthly data is not yet available** from any of the standard sources. The Ember Monthly Electricity Dataset (downloaded June 17, 2026) covers Turkey through **April 2026** only. The EPDK monthly sector report for May 2026 is not yet published (typically released with a 1-2 month lag, expected July 2026). Real-time hourly data is available on the EPIAS Transparency Platform (seffaflik.epias.com.tr) but requires browser/API access.

Below I present: (1) the most recent available data (Jan–Apr 2026), (2) May 2025 as the closest monthly comparison, and (3) multi-year May trends.

---

## 1. May 2025 Generation Data (Most Recent May Available)

**Source:** Ember Monthly Electricity Data (CSV downloaded 2026-06-17)

| Fuel Source | Generation (TWh) | Share (%) | YoY Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Coal** | **7.91** | **28.48%** | -4.35% |
| **Wind** | **2.78** | **10.01%** | +12.55% |
| **Solar** | **3.89** | **14.01%** | +56.85% |
| Hydro | 7.39 | 26.61% | -10.32% |
| Gas | 4.10 | 14.76% | +15.17% |
| Bioenergy | 0.72 | 2.59% | -4.00% |
| Other Renewables | 0.87 | 3.13% | +3.57% |
| Other Fossil | 0.11 | 0.40% | +22.22% |
| **Total Generation** | **27.77** | **100%** | **+4.01%** |

**Wind + Solar combined:** 6.67 TWh (24.02%), up +34.75% YoY.

---

## 2. January–April 2026 Monthly Data (Most Recent Available)

| Month | Total (TWh) | Coal (TWh) | Wind (TWh) | Solar (TWh) | Gas (TWh) | Hydro (TWh) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jan 2026 | 31.81 | 10.97 | 5.22 | 1.55 | 8.07 | 4.23 |
| Feb 2026 | 27.53 | 9.04 | 4.05 | 1.96 | 2.61 | 8.21 |
| Mar 2026 | 29.19 | 7.98 | 3.65 | 3.19 | 2.01 | 10.58 |
| Apr 2026 | 27.46 | 5.76 | 2.67 | 3.61 | 2.11 | 11.63 |
| **Jan-Apr Total** | **115.99** | **33.75** | **15.59** | **10.31** | **14.80** | **34.65** |

### Year-over-Year Changes (2026 vs. 2025):

| Month | Coal YoY | Wind YoY | Solar YoY | Total YoY |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jan 2026 | -0.27% | **+84.45%** | -7.19% | +5.33% |
| Feb 2026 | -8.50% | +32.79% | +9.50% | -2.41% |
| Mar 2026 | -14.01% | +5.19% | +10.38% | +4.18% |
| Apr 2026 | **-22.27%** | -5.32% | +17.59% | +2.92% |

**Key 2026 trend:** Coal generation declining sharply (especially in spring), wind surging (especially Jan–Feb), solar growing steadily, and hydro very strong (near-doubling in Feb–Apr, likely due to favorable precipitation).

---

## 3. May Coal Generation: Multi-Year Trend (2020–2025)

| Year | May Coal (TWh) | YoY Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2020 | 6.16 | -11.37% |
| 2021 | 7.39 | +19.97% |
| 2022 | 6.77 | -8.39% |
| 2023 | 7.77 | +14.77% |
| 2024 | 8.27 | +6.44% |
| 2025 | 7.91 | -4.35% |

Coal in May fluctuates between ~6–8 TWh. The 2026 spring trend shows a steep drop in coal generation (Apr 2026 was only 5.76 TWh, down 22% YoY), suggesting May 2026 coal could be lower than May 2025 as well. However, new domestic coal purchase guarantees at 75 USD/MWh (effective 2026) may offset this.

---

## 4. May Wind Generation: Multi-Year Trend (2020–2025)

| Year | May Wind (TWh) | YoY Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2020 | 1.53 | +31.90% |
| 2021 | 2.08 | +35.95% |
| 2022 | 2.09 | +0.48% |
| 2023 | 2.35 | +12.44% |
| 2024 | 2.47 | +5.11% |
| 2025 | 2.78 | +12.55% |

Wind showed steady growth. January 2026 saw a remarkable 84.45% YoY jump to 5.22 TWh, reflecting record 1.9 GW capacity additions in 2025.

---

## 5. May Solar Generation: Multi-Year Trend (2020–2025)

| Year | May Solar (TWh) | YoY Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2020 | 1.15 | +15.00% |
| 2021 | 1.47 | +27.83% |
| 2022 | 1.60 | +8.84% |
| 2023 | 1.81 | +13.13% |
| 2024 | 2.48 | +37.02% |
| 2025 | 3.89 | **+56.85%** |

Solar is the fastest-growing source. May 2025 solar nearly doubled from 2023 levels. Solar doubled in installed capacity from ~13 GW (end 2023) to ~26.7 GW (April 2026).

---

## 6. Installed Capacity (as of End April 2026)

**Source:** Turkish Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources (enerji.gov.tr, updated 21 May 2026)

| Source | Capacity (MW est.) | Share (%) |
|---|---|---|
| **Total** | **~125,410** | **100%** |
| Hydro | ~32,356 | 25.8% |
| **Solar** | **~26,712** | **21.3%** |
| Gas | ~24,831 | 19.8% |
| **Coal** | **~22,072** | **17.6%** |
| **Wind** | **~15,049** | **12.0%** |
| Geothermal | ~1,756 | 1.4% |
| Other | ~2,634 | 2.1% |

Solar is now the **single largest source by installed capacity** in Turkey, surpassing natural gas.

---

## 7. Full Year 2025 Context

**Source:** Ember Turkiye Electricity Review 2026 (published April 8, 2026) and Turkish Ministry of Energy

- Total 2025 generation: **362.9 TWh** (+2.4% YoY)
- Coal: **~121 TWh** (33.6%), down slightly from 2024 record of 122 TWh
- Wind: **~39.6 TWh** (10.9%), record 1.9 GW new capacity in 2025
- Solar: **~37.3–38.1 TWh** (10.5%), doubled from 2023 (18.4 TWh)
- Wind + Solar combined: **22%** of generation (record, first time exceeding hydro)

---

## 8. Where to Find May 2026 Data When Available

1. **EPIAS Transparency Platform** (real-time): https://seffaflik.epias.com.tr — hourly generation by source, aggregatable to monthly
2. **EPDK Monthly Sector Report** (expected July 2026): https://www.epdk.gov.tr/Detay/Icerik/3-0-23/elektrik-piyasasi-aylik-sektor-raporlari
3. **Ember Monthly Data** (next update likely July/Aug 2026): https://ember-energy.org/data/monthly-electricity-data/

key_facts: May 2026 monthly electricity generation data for Turkey is NOT YET AVAILABLE in any aggregated public dataset as of June 17, 2026; Ember data covers through April 2026 only
May 2025 total generation: 27.77 TWh (+4.01% YoY), with coal 7.91 TWh (28.5%), wind 2.78 TWh (10.0%), solar 3.89 TWh (14.0%)
May 2025 wind+solar combined: 6.67 TWh (24.0%), up 34.75% YoY
Jan-Apr 2026 coal generation declining sharply: 10.97→9.04→7.98→5.76 TWh (April down 22.3% YoY)
Jan 2026 wind generation surged to 5.22 TWh (+84.5% YoY), reflecting record 1.9 GW capacity additions in 2025
Jan-Apr 2026 solar generation growing steadily: 1.55→1.96→3.19→3.61 TWh
Turkey installed capacity as of end-April 2026: ~125.4 GW total; Solar ~26.7 GW (21.3%), Coal ~22.1 GW (17.6%), Wind ~15.0 GW (12.0%)
Solar is now Turkey's single largest source by installed capacity, surpassing natural gas
Full-year 2025: 362.9 TWh total generation; coal 33.6%, wind 10.9%, solar 10.5%; wind+solar at record 22%
Solar generation doubled from 18.4 TWh (2023) to 37.3 TWh (2025); May solar nearly quadrupled from 1.15 TWh (2020) to 3.89 TWh (2025)
New domestic coal purchase guarantee at 75 USD/MWh effective 2026 through 2029 may increase domestic coal output
EPDK May 2026 monthly sector report expected publication: July 2026
